key_insight: Detonation-sprayed Fe-based amorphous coatings with more disk-like, well-spread droplets form denser microstructures and deliver better long-term corrosion and wear performance, especially under higher humidity.
engineering_relevance: Optimizing spraying parameters to maximize droplet circularity and minimize porosity can improve coating durability for steel protection in corrosive and humid service environments.
method: Detonation spray Fe-based amorphous coatings onto Q235 carbon steel under different parameters
Compare coating microstructure, porosity, corrosion resistance, and tribological behavior
Test wear performance in varying humidity conditions with Si3N4 counterbodies
evidence_signal: The abstract reports systematic testing showing that coatings with fewer porosity defects have stronger long-term corrosion resistance, while friction coefficient and wear rate decrease as humidity increases.
watch_item: Performance is sensitive to spray conditions and microstructural defects, so process control is critical before scaling to real components.
paper_title: Long-term corrosion behaviors and wear behavior of Fe-based amorphous coatings in different humidity environments
